comment: Although this claim is impacted by the Gallop poll, it is still broadly true, and you'd need to find polls of multiple other countries to show that support for SSM has stalled or reversed to remove the claim entirely

/* */ Regarding this matter, a Gallup poll showed a slight decline in support in the United States in recent years: https://news.gallup.com/poll/691139/record-party-divide-years-sex-marriage-ruling.aspx https://www.them.us/story/support-for-gay-same-sex-marriage-survey-decrease-dropped-2025. Regarding the rest of the article, it seems clearly polemical and imprecise in its language

rv; per [[WP:LEAD]], the lead must reflect the body, so it's inappropriate to rewrite it so dramatically in a way that goes against it, especially when relying on the same sources. Beyond that, this is what the sources here broadly say. I'm not seeing anything on the linked wikipedia article contradicting it the way you said, either.

/* */ removed untrue claim, support has not consistently, continuously risen in recent years https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_opinion_of_same-sex_marriage_in_the_United_States. Also, I previously referred to the NFIS study - I was referring to the NFSS study. This whole article should be rewritten, but I'll submit these edits and see how they're recieved - I know some pages have a 'guard' on them so to speak, of people who enforce their own views related to the subject of it

/* */ Whether same-sex marriage harms heterosexual marriage as an institution is not scientifically quantifiable. One could argue rising support for polyamory as an example - regardless, it's not a scientific question. The rest reads as a barely disguised ideological polemic - what does posing no threat to 'civilization' mean? That's not something that can be shown by social science, far too imprecise terminology.

/* */ The NFIS study found discrepancies in outcomes re: children of same sex vs opposite sex couples. Its methodology was criticised, but sociologist Christian Smith argued that its flaws are shared by other studies arguing otherwise. These studies have a problem of self-selection - regardless, the use of 'refuted' is inappropriate in this instance, and the whole paragraph seems polemical and unnecessary.

/* Same-sex marriage around the world */This claim is questionable. It's been almost 20 years since the Czech Republic approved civil unions for same-sex couples in 2006. However progress on same-sex marriage has stalled. Hungary has recognized civil unions since 2009, yet the situation in that country has regressed significantly. Also, some countries like Canada, South Africa, Argentina, Cuba, Thailand had no nationwide civil union laws before legazlizing same sex marriage.

Updated for concision and accuracy. The timeline summary says when "sovereign states" legalized, or when "local laws were passed if marriages were already legal under higher authority." However, the prior version of the timeline had many subnational entities' date of legalization that were prior to the sovereign nation's legalization date. This version focuses on either sovereign states or local laws passed after sovereign states legalized at the national level.

The sentence must reflect nuance in the real world, or be stated as an opinion.
Removed sentence with "support" and "oppose".  Actual opinions are frequently more nuanced.
